title: Old Testament Deep Study
subtitle: Genesis Through Chronicles — Redemptive History in the Hebrew Scriptures
description: A systematic sixty-lesson study of the Old Testament from Genesis through Chronicles, tracing God's covenant faithfulness through creation, fall, exodus, law, kingship, and exile. Each lesson covers a specific book and chapter range with emphasis on canonical context and theological meaning.
learning_outcomes: Trace the covenant narrative from creation through the exile
Identify the major theological themes of each OT book studied
Explain the function of law, sacrifice, and priesthood in Israel's worship
Describe the Davidic covenant and its significance for OT and NT theology
Identify canonical connections between OT books and NT fulfillment

theological_framework.emphasis: God's covenant faithfulness across the Hebrew canon

units.title: Torah — Genesis
units.description: Creation, fall, flood, patriarchs, and Joseph (Lessons 1–12)

units.title: Torah — Exodus and Leviticus
units.description: Deliverance, Sinai covenant, tabernacle, and holiness law (Lessons 13–28)

units.title: Former Prophets — Joshua and Judges
units.description: Conquest, settlement, and the judge cycle (Lessons 43–50)

units.title: Historical Books — Ruth Through 2 Kings
units.description: Ruth, Samuel, and Kings — covenant faithfulness, kingship, and exile (Lessons 51–59)

units.title: Chronicles
units.description: Temple, worship, and Davidic hope in exile perspective (Lesson 60)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

questions: What three elements did God promise Abram in Genesis 12:1-3?
What is the name of the high priest's annual entry into the Most Holy Place?
What was the consequence of Israel's rebellion at Kadesh-Barnea?
What covenant did God make with David in 2 Samuel 7?
What is the primary concern of Chronicles compared to Samuel and Kings?
